---
title: "Philip Felix"
subtitle: "Senior Data Scientist"
body-classes: home-page
toc: false
---

::: {.hero-section}

# Philip Felix

::: {.hero-subtitle}
Senior Data Scientist — San Francisco
:::

::: {.hero-bio}
Currently building with LLMs and shipping side projects.
:::

:::

::: {.section-heading}
Projects
:::

::: {.project-grid}

::: {.project-card}
::: {.project-card-title}
[Job Title Normalizer](projects/title-normalizer.qmd)
:::
::: {.project-card-description}
CRM job titles are a mess. "Sr. Dir. Mktg (EMEA)" and "Senior Director of Marketing" are the same person. A multi-task PyTorch classifier that standardizes them into seniority levels and job functions, handling 15 categories of real-world CRM noise.
:::
::: {.project-card-stack}
Python · PyTorch · scikit-learn
:::
:::

::: {.project-card}
::: {.project-card-title}
[Adaptive Analyst Agent](projects/context-agent.qmd)
:::
::: {.project-card-description}
An LLM-powered analyst that answers the same business question differently depending on who's asking. A VP gets a board-level summary. A campaign manager gets channel attribution and budget recommendations. Same data, different context.
:::
::: {.project-card-stack}
Python · Claude API · DuckDB · Streamlit
:::
:::

::: {.project-card}
::: {.project-card-title}
[Support Ticket Classification](projects/support-ticket-classification.qmd)
:::
::: {.project-card-description}
Which classification method should you use — and how does the answer change based on how much labeled data you have? Seven methods compared across 100 to 100K tickets, from TF-IDF baselines to fine-tuned LLMs, on synthetic data modeled after OpenAI's help center.
:::
::: {.project-card-stack}
Python · Next.js · TypeScript · Recharts
:::
:::

::: {.project-card}
::: {.project-card-title}
[InterviewReady](projects/interview-ready.qmd)
:::
::: {.project-card-description}
Paste a job description and get a tailored practice session — technical coding questions, case studies, and behavioral questions calibrated to the role, company, and seniority level. AI-evaluated feedback with scoring and coaching.
:::
::: {.project-card-stack}
Next.js · Claude API · Supabase · Zustand
:::
:::

::: {.project-card}
::: {.project-card-title}
[Marketing Measurement Under Hypergrowth](projects/marketing-measurement.qmd)
:::
::: {.project-card-description}
When a company grows from $1B to $30B in 15 months on mostly organic adoption, every standard measurement approach breaks. An interactive app showing why attribution overcredits by 3-9x and how to build a measurement stack that works.
:::
::: {.project-card-stack}
Python · Next.js · TypeScript · Visx · Recharts
:::
:::

::: {.project-card}
::: {.project-card-title}
[Lead Scoring & Buying Group Identification](projects/lead-scoring.qmd)
:::
::: {.project-card-description}
You have 100K accounts and can only engage a few thousand this quarter. Which ones? A propensity model to answer that, plus a buying group analyzer that figures out why good accounts aren't closing and who's missing from the deal.
:::
::: {.project-card-stack}
Python · Pandas · scikit-learn · Streamlit
:::
:::

::: {.project-card}
::: {.project-card-title}
[AI Adoption Playbook](projects/ai-adoption-playbook.qmd)
:::
::: {.project-card-description}
A practical framework for getting a data science team from "what's an MCP server?" to LLM-assisted workflows. The actual steps, in order, with common blockers and what to do about them.
:::
::: {.project-card-stack}
Claude · MCP · Cursor · Databricks CLI
:::
:::

:::

::: {.contact-section}
I'm currently looking for senior data science roles. If you're hiring or just want to talk shop: [pfelix828@gmail.com](mailto:pfelix828@gmail.com)
:::
